titleVariants: Desktop Platform Engineer – Build, Release & Updates
Desktop Developer – UI Systems & State Management
Desktop App Developer – Windows + macOS Packaging
summaryVariants: Build fast, stable cross-platform desktop apps with Electron/Tauri and production-grade release workflows.
Own desktop features start-to-finish: UI, local storage, OS integrations, packaging, and auto-updates.
Design reliable Windows/macOS applications with strong performance, security, and crash resilience.
Ship polished desktop experiences with robust telemetry, diagnostics, and iterative improvements.
Build offline-first desktop apps with secure local data, sync, and background processes.
Create reusable desktop components, patterns, and build pipelines for rapid feature delivery.
Deliver native OS integrations (notifications, tray, file system, permissions) across platforms.
Improve startup time, memory footprint, and rendering performance using profiling and benchmarks.
Build desktop apps that feel native while keeping cross-platform velocity high.
Own the desktop platform foundation: packaging, signing, updates, and CI for Windows/macOS.
overviewVariants: You’ll build and maintain cross-platform desktop apps used by customers and internal teams. The role blends UI craft, system engineering, and release discipline.
This role focuses on delivering desktop applications that are secure, performant, and easy to ship—covering architecture, OS integrations, and stable releases.
You’ll work across Electron/Tauri and native modules where needed, collaborating with backend and UX to deliver a polished desktop product.
responsibilitySets: Develop and maintain cross-platform desktop apps using Electron.js or Tauri
Build core modules for local storage, auth, sync, and background tasks
Implement OS integrations (tray/menu, notifications, file system, deep links)
Design IPC patterns between UI and native/back-end layers
Write tests and add telemetry for reliability and diagnostics
Own packaging, signing, and distribution for Windows and macOS
Implement auto-update strategies (staged rollouts, rollback, versioning)
Build CI pipelines for desktop builds, artifact publishing, and release notes
Debug production issues using logs, crash dumps, and telemetry
Maintain documentation for builds, releases, and troubleshooting
Optimize startup time, memory, and rendering performance
Profile hotspots and remove bottlenecks (CPU, I/O, UI rendering)
Implement caching and offline-first strategies
Improve accessibility and UX consistency across OSes
Collaborate with design to deliver polished UI interactions
Integrate with backend APIs and secure local credential handling

Harden app security (code signing, sandboxing, permissions, secure IPC)
Perform dependency audits and patch vulnerabilities
Add guardrails for safe file handling and permissions
Build native modules where required (Rust/C++/Node addons)
Maintain cross-platform compatibility and OS-specific behaviors
Create reusable UI components and state management patterns
Ensure stability under network loss, sleep/wake, and low-resource conditions
Mentor engineers and lead code reviews for desktop components
Design modular architecture for feature delivery at scale
Define coding standards and quality gates for desktop apps
Build observability: structured logging, tracing, metrics
Coordinate releases with stakeholders and customer support
Investigate user feedback and drive fixes and improvements
Implement real-time features using WebSockets and background workers
Create robust error handling and retry strategies
Ensure smooth upgrades and data migrations between versions
Build automated smoke tests for critical flows
Own compatibility testing across OS versions
Develop cross-platform UI using web tech while keeping native feel
Implement theming, layout responsiveness, and keyboard shortcuts
Build a consistent design system implementation for desktop UI
Optimize bundling and asset loading
Collaborate with product for roadmap estimation and delivery
Handle file import/export, previews, and OS-level associations
Build sandboxed plugin-like modules if needed
Implement data sync conflict resolution strategies
Create internal tooling to speed up dev/release cycles
Contribute to engineering best practices and docs
Lead architecture decisions and technical planning
Mentor team members, onboard new hires, and conduct reviews
Ensure long-term maintainability and reduce technical debt
Coordinate security/performance reviews for major releases
Own roadmap for desktop platform evolution (Electron/Tauri/native)
requirementSets: 5+ years of software engineering experience with desktop app focus
Hands-on Electron.js (main/renderer, IPC) or Tauri (Rust + web UI)
Strong TypeScript/JavaScript fundamentals and debugging ability
Experience with packaging, signing, installers, and auto-updates
Comfort working with OS APIs (file system, permissions, notifications)
5+ years building production software; desktop experience preferred
Experience with Rust (Tauri) OR Node.js (Electron) OR C++ (Qt/native)
Solid understanding of app performance profiling and optimization
CI/CD familiarity for desktop build pipelines
Ability to design maintainable architectures and reusable components
Strong understanding of security basics for client apps (secrets, encryption, signing)
Experience handling offline mode, data migrations, and local persistence
Good knowledge of networking fundamentals and error handling
Experience working with REST APIs and auth flows
Strong communication and ownership mindset
Experience integrating native modules/addons (Rust/C++/Node)
Understanding of macOS notarization and Windows signing/distribution
Familiarity with crash reporting and telemetry systems
Experience with automated tests for desktop apps
Ability to collaborate with product and design
Experience leading features start-to-finish in a product team
Strong code review discipline and mentoring experience
Ability to prioritize, estimate, and deliver reliably
Comfort troubleshooting production issues under time pressure
Willingness to learn and adapt between Electron/Tauri/native
benefitsVariants: Flexible work model (role dependent), health insurance, learning budget, and high-ownership projects.
Competitive pay, paid time off, mentorship, and a strong engineering culture focused on quality.
Health coverage, growth opportunities, and a team that values craftsmanship and autonomy.
interviewProcessVariants: Intro call → Technical round → System/architecture discussion → Culture fit → Offer
Screening → Take-home/Live coding → Deep dive (desktop/release) → Final discussion
Recruiter chat → Technical → Problem-solving + debugging → Final round

summaryVariants: Create scroll-stopping short-form content that builds brand and drives measurable results.
Own content start-to-finish: ideation, scripting, shooting, editing, and publishing across platforms.
Turn product stories into engaging reels, posts, and campaigns that convert attention into trust.
Build a consistent, high-quality content engine across Instagram, YouTube Shorts, and LinkedIn.
Create high-volume, high-quality content with a sharp eye for storytelling and editing.
Lead a content calendar and collaborate with performance marketing to produce winning creatives.

Craft narratives and visuals that help the audience understand and love the brand.
Build a content pipeline that supports launches, events, and always-on social presence.
Create content assets that are reusable across paid ads, organic social, and website.
overviewVariants: You’ll create social-first content that reflects the brand voice, supports campaigns, and drives growth. You’ll collaborate with marketing, design, and leadership to keep the output consistent and high quality.
This role blends creativity with execution. You’ll ship content quickly, learn from performance, and iterate on formats that work.

Maintain platform-specific best practices and trend tracking

Produce product explainers, tutorials, and feature walkthroughs

Ensure content is accessible (captions, legibility, pacing)

Deliver fast variations: hooks, thumbnails, opening frames

Strong short-form editing skills (Premiere Pro / CapCut / VN / similar)

Ability to work fast without losing quality
